DMSTAr 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Changelog

DMSTA hydrology engine (internal)

Source: R/hydro.R
internal_dmsta_hydro.Rd

Internal helpers implementing DMSTA hydrologic integration, including within-day control depth interpolation, derivative evaluation, and RK4 stepping.

Internal helper to interpolate a daily control depth time series within a day using one of three DMSTA interpolation conventions:

  • 1: inputs treated as daily means (no within-day variation)

  • 2: inputs treated as mid-day values (DMSTA default)

  • 3: inputs treated as end-of-day values

Internal helper corresponding to one derivative evaluation (DMSTA "DerivFlow") used by the Runge-Kutta integrator. Given current volume and forcing inputs, computes net fluxes, outflow, seepage, bypass, and the instantaneous rate of change in volume (dvdt).

Internal helper that performs one Runge-Kutta 4th order (RK4) sub-step for hydrologic volume using dmsta_deriv_flow() for derivative evaluations.

Internal helper that integrates hydrologic volume and fluxes over one day using Nsteps RK4 sub-steps. Returns daily totals/diagnostics and (optionally) per-substep details in meta$steps.

Similar to dmsta_flow_day() but returns the per-substep mean rates explicitly in the steps element (useful for coupling to constituent transport/biogeochemistry that needs sub-daily hydrology).

dmsta_interp_zcontrol(
  Zcontrol1,
  Zcontrol,
  Zcontrol2,
  delta,
  interp_option = 2L
)

dmsta_deriv_flow(
  V,
  A_cell,
  Qi,
  Rain,
  Et,
  Qr0,
  Zcontrol_t,
  Zcontrol_t_minus,
  Zcontrol_t_plus,
  params,
  step_index,
  nsteps,
  step_frac,
  Ddt,
  Qrelease = 0,
  RecycleQ = 0,
  has_depth_constraint = FALSE,
  ...
)

dmsta_rk4_step(V, args_base, step_index, Dt)

dmsta_flow_day(V, inputs, params, Nsteps = 4)

dmsta_flow_day_steps(V, inputs, params, Nsteps = 4L)

Arguments

Zcontrol1

Numeric scalar. Control depth for yesterday.

Zcontrol

Numeric scalar. Control depth for today.

Zcontrol2

Numeric scalar. Control depth for tomorrow.

delta

Numeric scalar in \([0,1]\). Fraction of day elapsed.

interp_option

Integer. Interpolation convention (1, 2, or 3).

V

Numeric scalar. Starting volume for the day as hm3

A_cell

Numeric scalar. Cell area (e.g., km^2).

Qi

Numeric scalar. Inflow rate (e.g., hm^3/day).

Rain

Numeric scalar. Rainfall rate (e.g., m/day).

Et

Numeric scalar. Evapotranspiration rate (e.g., m/day).

Zcontrol_t

Numeric scalar. Today's control depth (m).

Zcontrol_t_minus

Numeric scalar. Yesterday's control depth (m).

Zcontrol_t_plus

Numeric scalar. Tomorrow's control depth (m).

params

Named list of parameters; see dmsta_flow_day().

step_index

Integer. Sub-step index in 1..nsteps.

nsteps

Integer. Total number of sub-steps per day.

step_frac

Numeric. RK stage fraction (0, 0.5, 0.5, 1).

Ddt

Numeric. Effective RK stage duration (days).

Qrelease

Numeric scalar. Optional release rate (hm^3/day).

RecycleQ

Numeric scalar. Optional recycle inflow (hm^3/day).

has_depth_constraint

Logical. Whether a depth-control series is present.

...

Reserved for future extensions.

args_base

Named list of arguments passed to dmsta_deriv_flow() via do.call(), typically containing Qi, Rain, Et, Zcontrol_t, neighbor control depths, params, nsteps, release components (Qr0, Qr1, Qr2), and flags.

Dt

Numeric scalar. Sub-step duration in days (1/nsteps).

inputs

Named list of forcings; see dmsta_flow_day().

Nsteps

Integer. Number of sub-steps per day.

Value

Numeric scalar interpolated control depth for the within-day time point.

A named list with instantaneous fluxes and diagnostics:

dvdt

Net dV/dt.

Qo

Outflow rate excluding Qrelease.

Qout

Total outflow rate including Qrelease.

Etest

Effective ET rate used after any shutdown logic.

Seepout, Seepin

Seepage rates.

Bypass

Bypass flow rate.

Z

Current depth estimate V/A_cell.

Zcont

Interpolated control depth used for this stage.

A named list with elements:

V_new

Updated volume (clamped to minimum volume).

Qout_ts

Stage-averaged total outflow rate (including releases).

SeepOut_ts, SeepIn_ts

Stage-averaged seepage rates.

Bypass_ts

Stage-averaged bypass rate.

Etest_ts

Stage-averaged ET rate used.

Q_treated_ts

Portion of outflow assigned to the treated component.

Q_rel1_ts, Q_rel2_ts

Portions of outflow assigned to release components.

A structured list with components:

results

Named list of daily totals and end-of-day state (e.g., V_end, Qout, seepage, bypass).

budgets

Named lists for water budget diagnostics (mass budget is NULL for hydrology-only).

meta

Inputs used, parameters used, and per-substep records.

A named list including:

V_end

End-of-day volume.

steps

List of length Nsteps with per-substep mean rates and states.

Qout, SeepOut, SeepIn, Bypass, RainVol, EtVol, NetAtmo

Daily totals.

WB_in, WB_out, WB_err, WB_rel

Water balance diagnostics.

Details

Missing values are handled defensively:

  • if today's Zcontrol is NA, it is treated as 0

  • if neighbor days are NA, they fall back to today's value

The interpolation fraction delta is clamped to \([0,1]\).

Control depth is interpolated within the day via dmsta_interp_zcontrol() using the RK stage timing defined by step_index, nsteps, and step_frac.

Returns the updated volume (with safety clamp to minimum volume) along with stage-averaged fluxes for outflow, seepage, bypass, and ET.

Contents

Developed by Paul Julian.
